does: This work provides a systematic practicum covering the complete modern NLP pipeline from tokenisation through reinforcement learning from human feedback, with particular emphasis on practical implementation and low-resource languages. The authors develop original contributions for Tajik and Tatar—including subword tokenisers, word embeddings, lexical databases, and transliteration benchmarks—demonstrating how contemporary NLP techniques can be adapted to morphologically rich, data-scarce settings. Each of the twelve sessions combines theoretical foundations with detailed implementation guidance, formalised evaluation metrics, and transparent assessment criteria, structured as a reproducible research artefact where code, models, and reports must be published in open repositories and all experiments use a single evolving corpus.
connects: This work responds to the growing gap between cutting-edge NLP research focused on high-resource languages and the practical needs of low-resource language communities. It builds on decades of tokenisation theory and modern subword approaches like BPE and SentencePiece, extends work on cross-lingual word embeddings and transfer learning, and incorporates recent advances in large language model fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented generation. The emphasis on morphologically rich languages connects to established research on morphological analysis and agglutinative language processing, while the RLHF section engages with recent work on aligning language models with human preferences through systematic feedback mechanisms.
enables: By providing reproducible, transparent methodology for adapting modern NLP to low-resource settings, this work enables future researchers to develop tools and models for underrepresented languages with clear implementation paths and established benchmarks. The public artifacts—code, models, and evaluation frameworks—create reusable components for Tajik and Tatar that can serve as templates for other morphologically rich languages. The systematic pipeline from raw text to aligned language models provides a foundation for scaling NLP capabilities to languages currently lacking in computational resources, potentially accelerating the development of inclusive language technologies and democratising access to modern NLP techniques across linguistic communities.
